SEARCH
Package opennlp

Class OpenNLPServer

java.lang.Object
opennlp.OpenNLPServer
All Implemented Interfaces:
Callable<Integer>
public class OpenNLPServer extends Object implements Callable<Integer>
The OpenNLPServer class implements a gRPC server for providing OpenNLP-based services. It is a command-line application that allows configuration through command-line options and a configuration file. The server hosts services such as POS tagging using OpenNLP models, and can optionally enable reflection for gRPC clients.

This server listens on a configurable port (default is 7071). It loads configuration settings from a configured file (if present, otherwise falls back to defaults) and uses them to initialize various components such as the POS tagger service.

Command-line Options:

  • -p, --port: Specifies the port on which the server should listen (default is 7071).
  • -c, --config: Specifies the path to a configuration file, which contains key-value pairs.

Service Initialization: The server reads the configuration file (if provided) and uses it to load various settings. It then starts the OpenNLP service, including loading models and initializing the POS tagger service.

The server can also enable reflection for gRPC, which allows clients to introspect the available services at runtime. This is done by setting server.enable_reflection to true.

Lifecycle Management: The server includes graceful shutdown handling using a shutdown hook to ensure resources are released when the server is stopped.
